comment.body:
I have been thinking in a business mentality. The very nature of you calling it a “Holy Alliance 2.0” (which was the first?) signifies a deeper connection of these country’s values outside of strictly economical. You wrote about that when you first introduced it.

Now, which has always seemed to me to be more logically, it is about these country’s own self interests, and playing both sides of this US/China conflict to their advantage according to which one they can impose the most leverage on. Similar position to India.

You then ended the post that we are in a 50/50 split here. So what am I missing?

comment.body:
50/50 is for the market.

the holy alliance is an offer.
they have common ideological roots now, but a past full of conflicts. this is why the negotiations in jeedah are moving slowly.
but as much as i hear, they are moving forward.
but until then the countries keep their businesses running, and it is brics.

now, the investors want Trump to fight stagflation, then they will save the economy by buying debt, and tech etc.
but now the uncertainity is very high about the outcomes.
these games... so... nothing is above these plp in terms of power, so everything depends on their decisions and miscalculations.

I don't understand either fully why they are surprised: they asked for it, they knew that recession is coming, they knew that this will be like this, and we have one week until Easter.
but they lose a lot of money. Investors are net long on the economy. Not short. And they lose a lot of money.

And there can be a lot of factors that play out differently in the near-future. Factors that nobody could have calculated forward properly (in terms of probability).
and if the party is better in the other house, they will join that one. 

they don't allies yet!
